_comment: Canonical source roster for the Daily Cyber Briefing. The 6 AM scheduled task reads this file to build the briefing and APPENDS newly vetted sources here over time. Categories: breaking | investigative | threatintel | vulnfeeds | aisecurity. Tiers: core | strong | aifocus. Do not remove the seeded core sources without reason.

discoveryRules.criteria: Add only sources that are (1) reputable and editorially credible, (2) primarily original reporting/research rather than aggregation, (3) actively publishing in 2026, (4) clearly relevant to cybersecurity or AI/LLM security, and (5) not already present (match by domain). Prefer primary sources that break original stories. When unsure, log as a candidate instead of adding.

sources.name: The Hacker News
sources.note: Highest-volume breaking cyber news.

sources.name: BleepingComputer
sources.note: Best for ransomware/malware specifics and IOCs.

sources.name: Dark Reading
sources.note: Deep analysis across threat intel and vulns.

sources.name: SecurityWeek
sources.note: Strong on ICS/OT and enterprise threats.

sources.name: The Record (Recorded Future)
sources.note: Original nation-state, ransomware, policy reporting.

sources.name: CyberScoop
sources.note: Policy + breaking incidents, US gov/critical infra.

sources.name: Cybersecurity Dive
sources.note: Business-focused breach/regulation coverage.

sources.name: Ars Technica Security
sources.note: Technical how/why of zero-days and nation-state attacks.

sources.name: Infosecurity Magazine
sources.note: Industry news, M&A, vendor/tooling moves.

sources.name: Krebs on Security
sources.note: PRIMARY: original cybercrime investigations.

sources.name: Schneier on Security
sources.note: Systems-level analysis linking tech to policy.

sources.name: Risky Business
sources.note: Sharp weekly news + analysis; strong signal.

sources.name: The Last Watchdog
sources.note: Interview-driven analysis of emerging threats.

sources.name: Google / Mandiant Threat Intelligence
sources.note: Frontline incident research; M-Trends.

sources.name: Cisco Talos
sources.note: Largest commercial threat-intel team.

sources.name: Unit 42 (Palo Alto)
sources.note: Incident-response insights and threat research.

sources.name: Microsoft Threat Intelligence
sources.note: MSTIC nation-state and large-campaign research.

sources.name: CrowdStrike
sources.note: Adversary tracking; eCrime/nation-state intel.

sources.name: Sophos X-Ops
sources.note: Practical malware/ransomware research.

sources.name: ESET WeLiveSecurity
sources.note: Award-winning research; strong on APTs.

sources.name: SANS Internet Storm Center
sources.note: Daily handler diaries; early exploitation warnings.

sources.name: Google Project Zero
sources.note: PRIMARY: deep zero-day/exploit research.

sources.name: CISA KEV Catalog
sources.note: Authoritative actively-exploited CVE list. NOTE: CISA retired RSS May 2025; use JSON/CSV.

sources.name: NIST NVD
sources.note: Canonical CVE database with CVSS.

sources.name: CISA Advisories
sources.note: Government and ICS advisories.

sources.name: Rapid7 Blog
sources.note: Fast emergent-threat writeups on exploited CVEs.

sources.name: Zero Day Initiative
sources.note: Disclosures and Patch Tuesday breakdowns.

sources.name: Microsoft MSRC
sources.note: Microsoft advisories and Patch Tuesday guidance.

sources.name: OWASP GenAI Security Project
sources.note: Top 10 for LLMs and Agentic AI; risk guidance.

sources.name: MITRE ATLAS
sources.note: Adversarial AI tactics/techniques knowledge base.

sources.name: NIST AI RMF
sources.note: Governance/risk framework for AI systems.

sources.name: Lakera Blog
sources.note: Practical LLM-security research and red-teaming.

sources.name: Embrace The Red
sources.note: PRIMARY: hands-on AI exploitation research.

sources.name: Simon Willison's Weblog
sources.note: Plain-English prompt-injection and LLM security.

sources.name: Prompt Security
sources.note: Enterprise GenAI threat research and defenses.

sources.name: Lasso Security
sources.note: LLM/agent security insights and incident analysis.

sources.name: AI Security Newsletter (Tal Eliyahu)
sources.note: Monthly AI-security research/report digest.

sources.name: AISecHub Adversarial AI Digest
sources.note: Red-teaming and LLM exploitation roundups.

sources.name: Hugging Face Blog
sources.note: Model/dataset supply-chain risk posts.

sources.name: Import AI (Jack Clark)
sources.note: Weekly AI governance and safety context.

sources.name: The CyberWire
sources.note: Major daily cyber news/podcast network.
sources.rationale: Long-running, credible daily outlet with original reporting and clear summaries; surprising gap in the seed roster.

sources.name: Help Net Security
sources.note: Original news + research coverage, incl. AI-security incidents.
sources.rationale: Appeared as the original source on the LiteLLM PyPI backdoor and OWASP prompt-injection coverage; consistent original reporting since 1998.

sources.name: NVIDIA AI Red Team
sources.note: Practical LLM security research (garak, NeMo Guardrails).
sources.rationale: Primary AI-security research from a credible red team (Derczynski, Galinkin, Harang); fills a hands-on AI attack/defense gap.

sources.name: Security Affairs
sources.note: Pierluigi Paganini's long-running original-reporting blog.
sources.rationale: Promoted from candidate; was the original/early source on the Aflac 22.7M notification and the Iran-linked Handala Cal Water breach. Consistent original reporting since 2011.

sources.name: Obsidian Security
sources.note: SaaS/identity and AI-gateway security research.
sources.rationale: Primary research team that discovered and disclosed the LiteLLM CVSS 9.9 privilege-escalation/RCE chain (CVE-2026-47101/47102/40217); credible hands-on AI-infrastructure vuln research.

sources.name: CERT Polska (CERT.PL)
sources.note: Poland's national CERT; original APT/phishing analysis with IOCs.
sources.rationale: Was the original source for the Ghostwriter/UNC1151 Gmail 2FA-phishing campaign; credible government CSIRT publishing primary technical writeups.

sources.name: OX Security
sources.note: AppSec/AI supply-chain research team.
sources.rationale: Disclosed the systemic MCP arbitrary-command-execution vulnerability class affecting 150M+ downloads; original hands-on AI supply-chain research not yet in roster.

sources.name: Adversa AI
sources.note: Adversarial-ML / AI red-teaming research and MCP security tracking.
sources.rationale: Primary adversarial-AI research firm; surfaced the June MCP exposure data (Censys counts, VIPER-MCP 67 CVEs). Original technical analysis distinct from existing roster.

sources.name: Arctic Wolf Labs
sources.note: IR/threat-research team with original exploitation analysis and IOCs.
sources.rationale: Published primary technical analysis of the actively-exploited Oracle PeopleSoft CVE-2026-35273 (ShinyHunters) campaign ahead of broad coverage; credible incident-response research distinct from existing roster.

sources.name: ReliaQuest
sources.note: GreyMatter threat-research team; dark-web monitoring and true-positive incident analysis.
sources.rationale: Published the original Threat Spotlight tying the Klue/'Icarus' OAuth integration abuse to Salesforce CRM data theft ahead of broad coverage; consistent original research (SAP NetWeaver zero-day, annual threat report) distinct from existing roster.

sources.name: Huntress
sources.note: MDR provider with a hands-on intrusion-analysis blog and adversary-tactics library.
sources.rationale: Published its own primary investigation of the Klue breach (as both responder and victim) and consistent original intrusion writeups (Nightmare-Eclipse, Railway.com device-code phishing); credible first-party threat research not yet in roster.

sources.name: Orca Security
sources.note: Cloud/AI security research team with original supply-chain analysis.
sources.rationale: Published primary technical breakdown of the Mastra npm scope takeover (144 packages, easy-day-js typosquat, postinstall RAT) used by AI-agent builders; original AI supply-chain research distinct from existing roster.

sources.name: Cybernews
sources.note: Research team that discovers exposed databases and credential leaks first-hand.
sources.rationale: Its own researchers discovered and published today's lead story — the 24-billion-record, 8.3 TB infostealer leak — and have a documented track record of original breach-discovery research (4B-record China leak, IDMerit 3B KYC exposure). Primary investigative research, not aggregation; no cybernews.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: JFrog Security Research
sources.note: Vulnerability-research team that discloses original CVEs and writes working exploit walkthroughs.
sources.rationale: Published the first public root exploit walkthrough for the DirtyClone Linux kernel LPE (CVE-2026-43503) behind today's story; documented original-research track record (PixelSmash FFmpeg RCE CVE-2026-8461, Redis RCE, 183+ disclosed CVEs, malicious Hugging Face models). Primary technical research, not aggregation; no jfrog.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: Pillar Security
sources.note: AI/agent security research team with hands-on offensive findings.
sources.rationale: Published the original 'Agent Security Paradox' research disclosing the Cursor allowlist-bypass RCE (CVE-2026-22708) behind today's AI story; team has offensive-security/threat-intel background and consistent original agentic-AI attack-surface research. Primary research, not aggregation; no pillar.security domain in the roster.

sources.name: watchTowr Labs
sources.note: Offensive-research team that reverse-engineers patches and ships working PoCs for edge/enterprise CVEs.
sources.rationale: Published the original patch-diff analysis and working pre-auth PoC for today's lead vuln, Progress Kemp LoadMaster CVE-2026-8037, ahead of broad coverage; documented original-research track record on Citrix NetScaler and prior MOVEit-class edge bugs. Primary technical research, not aggregation; no watchtowr.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: XLab (QiAnXin)
sources.note: Botnet-hunting research lab; discovers and tracks large-scale botnets with C2 takeovers and telemetry.
sources.rationale: Discovered and published the original research on today's RustDuck botnet; renowned botnet-tracking team that has exposed 30+ globally impactful botnets (Zergeca, Vo1d, AISURU, AIRASHI, Kimwolf). Primary original research with hands-on C2/honeypot data; no qianxin.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: SentinelOne (SentinelLABS)
sources.note: SentinelLABS research team; original macOS/APT malware analysis and attribution.
sources.rationale: Published the original technical breakdown of today's macOS.Gaslight DPRK backdoor (Phil Stokes) — the first malware seen prompt-injecting AI triage agents. Consistent primary macOS/DPRK research (NimDoor, BONZAI family); no sentinelone.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: Calif.io
sources.note: Vulnerability-research team that discloses original CVEs, including AI-assisted discoveries with PoCs.
sources.rationale: Discovered and published the original disclosure and PoC for today's Squidbleed (CVE-2026-47729), a 29-year-old Squid proxy heap over-read found with AI assistance (Claude Mythos Preview); responsibly coordinated with the Squid project. Primary vuln research, not aggregation; no calif.io domain in the roster.

sources.name: SOCRadar
sources.note: Threat Research Unit (STRU) that runs original dark-web and exposure investigations and ships free checker tools.
sources.rationale: Its STRU team ran the original FortiBleed investigation behind today's lead story — analyzing the full 86,644-firewall dataset, first tying the campaign to INC and Lynx ransomware, notifying affected orgs and pushing data to CERTs. Primary original research, not aggregation; no socradar.io domain in the roster.

sources.name: Security Discovery (Bob Diachenko)
sources.note: Researcher who first-hand discovers exposed databases and misconfigured servers, often ahead of broad coverage.
sources.rationale: Bob Diachenko first flagged the exposed FortiBleed attacker server behind today's ransomware-pipeline story, and has a long documented track record of original breach discovery (MOAB 26B records, 8.7B-record Chinese leak, 4.3B database). Primary investigative discovery, not aggregation; no securitydiscovery.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: Sysdig (Threat Research Team)
sources.note: Cloud/runtime threat-research team (Sysdig TRT) that captures live attacks with honeypot/runtime telemetry and publishes original incident breakdowns with IOCs.
sources.rationale: Its TRT published the original research on today's lead AI story — JADEPUFFER, the first documented end-to-end LLM-driven ransomware operation (Langflow CVE-2025-3248 to database extortion) — with full payload analysis, timestamps and IOCs. Documented original-research track record (LLMjacking coinage, Langflow CVE-2026-33017 campaign, agentic container escape). Primary hands-on research, not aggregation; no sysdig.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: Blackpoint Cyber
sources.note: MDR provider with an APG (Adversary Pursuit Group) that publishes first-party intrusion analysis and malware breakdowns.
sources.rationale: Its researchers (Nevan Beal, Sam Decker) discovered and published the original analysis of today's Avalon modular framework and the new CrownX ransomware — full kill chain, EDR-evasion set and AI-assisted-development assessment — ahead of broad coverage. Primary threat research, not aggregation; no blackpointcyber.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: runZero Research
sources.note: Exposure-management research team (led by ex-CISA vulnerability chief Tod Beardsley) that discloses original CVEs in widely-embedded components and runs its own CNA.
sources.rationale: Discovered and published the original disclosure of today's seven FatFs embedded-filesystem flaws affecting millions of RTOS devices (cameras, drones, ATMs, crypto wallets); documented original-research track record (SSHamble SSH research, runZero CNA, structured 60-day CERT/CC disclosure). Primary vuln research with deep protocol/fingerprint analysis, not aggregation; no runzero.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: Ransom-ISAC
sources.note: Ransomware-focused ISAC that reconstructs extortion cases first-hand from leaked negotiation chats and on-chain payment tracing.
sources.rationale: Rakesh Krishnan's original Ransom-ISAC case study behind today's lead story reconstructed the Kairos ~$1M data-theft extortion of a U.S. county (Union County, Ohio) from a leaked negotiation chat and the Bitcoin trail (Bybit/OKX/BELQI), ahead of broad coverage; consistent original leak-analysis research (The Gentlemen ransomware leak analysis). Primary investigative research, not aggregation; no ransom-isac.org domain in the roster.

sources.name: Sekoia TDR
sources.note: Sekoia Threat Detection & Research team; original malware/campaign analysis with IOCs and infrastructure tracking.
sources.rationale: Its TDR team (with YesWeHack) ran the original investigation behind today's ChocoPoC story — analyzing seven trojanized CVE-PoC repos, the PyPI dropper chain and the Mapbox DNS-over-HTTPS dead-drop C2 — ahead of broad coverage; long-running French threat-intel firm with consistent primary research (Aurora Stealer, GootLoader, ClickFix tracking). Primary original research, not aggregation; no sekoia.io domain in the roster.

sources.name: Armadin
sources.note: AI-native offensive-security firm (ex-Mandiant leadership) whose researchers publish hands-on agent/AI-tool attack chains.
sources.rationale: Nick McClendon's original research behind today's AI story detailed a full sandbox escape reaching root inside Claude Cowork's Hyper-V VM (USERENV.dll sideload into signed claude.exe + CoworkVMService RPC abuse), responsibly disclosed to Anthropic; the firm is led by Kevin Mandia and Mandiant founders and focuses on autonomous AI red-teaming. Primary offensive AI research, not aggregation; no armadin.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: Zscaler ThreatLabz
sources.note: Zscaler's threat-research org; analyzes cloud telemetry at scale and runs its own AI-agent red-team experiments.
sources.rationale: Published the original indirect-prompt-injection research behind today's lead AI story — CSS-hidden and JSON-LD-buried web prompts that tricked 4 of 26 tested LLM agents (incl. Llama and Gemini variants) into autonomous crypto payments and typosquat trust — tested with its own in-house agent harness across SEO-poisoned sites. Documented track record of zero-day discoveries and annual ransomware/AI-security reports. Primary original research, not aggregation; no zscaler.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: Synacktiv
sources.note: French offensive-security/pentest firm that discloses original CVEs and unauthenticated RCE chains with deep technical writeups.
sources.rationale: Discovered and published the original unauthenticated-RCE research behind today's lead vuln story — an unpatched, un-CVE'd Argo CD repo-server flaw that can fully compromise a Kubernetes cluster — after 18 months of unresolved vendor disclosure; documented track record of 300+ public vulnerability disclosures (Livewire, BizTalk360, Snipe-IT, Citrix Provisioning Services) and Pwn2Own research. Primary technical research, not aggregation; no synacktiv.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: Aikido Security
sources.note: AI-assisted AppSec/pentesting vendor that discloses original CVEs with deep technical write-ups.
sources.rationale: Promoted from candidate: a month after its original decade-old phpBB auth-bypass discovery (CVE-2026-48611), published a full technical write-up plus a second related CVE (CVE-2026-48612) on July 3, honoring phpBB's requested 4-week embargo. Sustained cadence and embargo discipline over ~3 weeks resolves the 2026-06-16 'watch for independence' concern; primary vuln research, not aggregation.

sources.name: Proofpoint
sources.note: Threat Research team publishing original APT/campaign analysis with IOCs and attribution.
sources.rationale: Its Threat Research team has tracked UNK_MassTraction, a suspected China-aligned cluster exploiting Roundcube n-day flaws (CVE-2024-42009, CVE-2025-49113) against US/Canadian university physics and national-security researchers, since May 2026 — original hands-on campaign research (IceCube stealer, VShell backdoor analysis) ahead of broad coverage. Long-established, well-resourced threat-intel vendor with a deep original-research track record; no proofpoint.com domain in the roster.

sources.name: Varonis Threat Labs
sources.note: Data-security research team with a sustained track record of original AI-platform and SaaS vulnerability disclosures.
sources.rationale: Published the original 'Rogue Agent' research disclosing a shared-execution-environment flaw in Google Dialogflow CX (reported Nov 2025, fixed Jun 2026) that could let one edit permission compromise every AI chatbot agent in a GCP project. Consistent original AI-security disclosure cadence (SearchLeak in Microsoft 365 Copilot, Reprompt in Copilot Personal, Cookie Bite Azure MFA bypass). Primary hands-on research, not aggregation; no varonis.com domain in the roster.

candidates.name: CyberGeoDigest
candidates.rationale: Daily cyber+geopolitics briefing; useful but more of a curated digest than original reporting — watch for original analysis before promoting.

candidates.name: S-RM
candidates.rationale: Weekly cyber-intelligence briefing with some original incident framing (e.g., law-firm helpdesk-impersonation campaign). Useful, but largely a curated briefing — watch for sustained original reporting before promoting.

candidates.name: Halcyon
candidates.rationale: Original ransomware tracking (e.g., INC Ransom law-firm campaign, 200+ legal-sector incidents). Credible data, but a vendor alert feed — monitor independence/cadence before promoting.

candidates.name: YesWeHack
candidates.rationale: Its vulnerability-intelligence team co-authored the original ChocoPoC research with Sekoia TDR (trojanized CVE-PoC repos targeting researchers). Promising original vuln-intel, but primarily a bug-bounty-platform news blog — watch for sustained independent original research beyond co-published work before promoting.
